mapping.a_element: small-scale, low-amplitude surface density fluctuations in the lensing cluster (dark-matter minihalos / intracluster stars)
mapping.b_element: the quasiperiodic (or Anderson) disorder potential imposed on the unitary map

brief_justification: Only the generic notion of 'small-scale disorder/fluctuations' overlaps; the governing machinery differs decisively. Paper A is gravitational-lensing ray optics — caustic/micro-caustic formation and magnification statistics whose lightcurves probe density perturbations — whereas Paper B is localization theory of a nonlinear quasiperiodic unitary map, tracking Lyapunov-spectrum scaling versus localization length. There is no role-for-role correspondent for the lensing caustic network, nor for the Lyapunov exponent / localization-length crossover, so no coherent shared process holds.
